text: The role of minimally invasive surgery for management of small bowel obstruction. Small bowel obstruction is one of the common surgical emergency conditions. The video demonstrate the feasibility and safety of laparoscopic approach for the treatment of small bowel obstruction.
The first case was an eighty-seven-year-old male presented with abdominal pain and vomiting. CT scan show dilatation of jejunum with abrupt change in caliber at proximal ileum.

The surgeon inserts a visualized trocar port to safely access the abdomen, revealing the peritoneum. During diagnostic laparoscopy, a single adhesion band is identified, extending from the transverse colon to the mesentery, causing obstruction at the proximal ileum.

The surgeon performs sharp dissection using scissors to safely lyse the adhesion band. The entire intestine is inspected from the duodenal junction to the ileocecal valve, ensuring no heat damage to the bowel.

The second case was a sixty three year old female presented with abdominal distension and vomiting. She has a past history total abdominal hysterectomy ten years ago. CT scan show dilatation of small bowel with transition point in the terminal ileum.

The surgeon encounters a complex adhesion between the omentum and the abdominal wall. Trocar ports are strategically placed away from previous surgical scars to minimize the risk of injury to intra-abdominal organs.

During the diagnostic laparoscopy, multiple adhesion bands are identified as the cause of obstruction. The surgeon excises these bands using cold scissors, while optimal retraction of the bowel is maintained with an atraumatic grasper. Energy devices are avoided to prevent thermal injury.

Following the release of the adhesion band, the surgeon inspects the entire intestine to identify any additional obstruction points or undetected bowel injuries. The laparoscopic view shows the intestine being carefully examined for signs of injury or obstruction.

laparoscopic approach is associated with minimal post-operative pain shorter hospital stay and lower risk of post-operative morbidity conclusion laparoscopic approach for small bowel obstruction is safe feasible and effective the careful pre-operative evaluation and safe surgical technique play a role to improve the treatment outcomes thank you for the attention
